Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Understand the Goal The objective is to compare an event log 5 cases against a provided textual normative process description I need to identify which cases deviate from the rules and explain why based on the text

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Analyze the Normative Process Description Start Must begin with Receive order Step 2 Check inventory must happen before payment authorization or fulfillment decisions Branching Logic Scenario A Sufficient Inventory Receive Check Authorize Payment Package Items Ship Order Close Order Scenario B Insufficient Inventory Receive Check Cancel Order Close Order Constraints Rules Authorize payment After Check inventory Before Package items Package items After Authorize payment Before Ship order Ship order After Package items Never after Cancel order Close order Final activity in all scenarios Cancel order Should lead to Close order no fulfillment activities Package Ship after cancellation

Name: Abductive Reasoning - IND
Text: Ambiguities The text acknowledges some room for interpretation but emphasizes the core logic Inventory check before payment Cancellation stops fulfillment

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Case 1 Sequence Receive Check Authorize Package Ship Close Comparison Matches Scenario A perfectly Verdict Compliant

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Case 2 Sequence Receive Check Package Authorize Ship Close Comparison Receive OK Check OK Package happens before Authorize Rule Authorize payment should occur before packaging Rule Package items should only occur after authorization Verdict Violation

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Case 3 Sequence Receive Check Authorize Cancel Ship Close Comparison Receive OK Check OK Authorize OK Cancel happens Ship happens after Cancel Rule Ship order should never occur after a cancellation Rule Once canceled it would be illogical to continue with activities intended for fulfillment such as packaging or shipping Also Authorize payment happened before Cancel While the text says if inventory is insufficient go to Cancel here it authorized then canceled The most glaring violation is Shipping after Canceling Verdict Violation

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Case 4 Sequence Receive Check Cancel Close Comparison Matches Scenario B perfectly Verdict Compliant

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Case 5 Sequence Receive Check Authorize Ship Close Comparison Receive OK Check OK Authorize OK Ship happens Missing Package items Rule Package items should occur after authorization and before shipping Only after items have been properly packaged is it appropriate to proceed with Ship order Verdict Violation

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Synthesize Findings Violating Cases 2 3 5 Compliant Cases 1 4

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - IND
Text: Draft the Explanations Case 2 Focus on the order of Authorization vs Packaging The text explicitly states payment authorization must happen before packaging Case 3 Focus on the Cancel to Ship transition The text states shipping should never occur after cancellation Also the flow suggests a mixed state Authorized then Cancelled then Shipped which contradicts the branching logic Sufficient fulfill or Insufficient cancel Case 5 Focus on the missing Package items step The text states items must be prepared packaged before shipping

Name: Backtracking - PE
Text: Refine the Analysis Self Correction Edge Case Check Case 3 Nuance Is authorizing payment before canceling a violation The text says If the inventory check reveals that there is not enough stock the process should directly proceed to Cancel order In Case 3 they Check Authorize Cancel This implies they authorized payment despite potentially insufficient stock or they decided to cancel for another reason However the definitive violation is Ship order never after a cancellation I will focus on the Shipping after Cancellation as the primary violation Case 5 Nuance Is it possible to ship without packaging The text says Only after items have been properly packaged is it appropriate to proceed with Ship order This is a hard constraint Skipping Package items is a violation

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Case 1 OK Case 2 Violation Package before Authorize Case 3 Violation Ship after Cancel Case 4 OK Case 5 Violation Ship without Package
